Row1.Rationale: NTRK fusions generate constitutively active TRK kinases that drive oncogenesis; larotrectinib is a highly selective ATP‑competitive pan‑TRK inhibitor with low‑nanomolar potency, and TRK fusion–positive NSCLC shows high response rates when this pathway is inhibited, indicating TRK addiction.

Row2.Rationale: Constitutively active TRK fusion proteins upregulate MAPK signaling, promoting proliferation; schematic models and functional data show that TRK inhibition suppresses MAPK output, which aligns with durable responses to larotrectinib across NTRK‑rearranged tumors including NSCLC.

Row3.Rationale: TRK activation engages PI3K/AKT signaling in fusion‑positive cells, supporting survival and growth; inhibition of TRK reduces PI3K/AKT activity, and PI3K pathway activation is a recognized oncogenic driver in NSCLC, so its suppression contributes to larotrectinib sensitivity in NTRK‑rearranged lung cancer.

Row4.Rationale: Physiologic TRK receptors signal via PLCγ, and TRK fusion schematics show PLCγ pathway upregulation; larotrectinib blocks upstream TRK activity, thereby attenuating PLCγ‑dependent survival and differentiation signals that form part of the oncogenic program in NTRK‑fusion NSCLC.

Row5.Rationale: Reviews of TRK and TrkC biology indicate that aberrant TRK activation can signal through JAK/STAT3 to enhance tumorigenicity; by inhibiting TRK, larotrectinib is expected to diminish STAT3‑mediated transcriptional programs that support proliferation and metastasis in TRK‑driven lung tumors.

Row6.Rationale: Basket trials of larotrectinib across ≥16 tumor types show an ORR ~75–79% independent of histology, with NSCLC patients achieving similar response rates, indicating that suppression of a common fusion‑driven signaling network (MAPK, PI3K, PLCγ, JAK/STAT) is sufficient for tumor control in lung cancer.

Row7.Rationale: Larotrectinib demonstrates activity in patients with TRK fusion–positive tumors and brain metastases, and early trials included metastatic primary lung cancers; its ability to cross the blood–brain barrier and inhibit TRK in CNS lesions underlies intracranial responses in NTRK‑rearranged NSCLC.

Row8.Rationale: Acquired NTRK1/3 kinase‑domain mutations at gatekeeper (F589L), solvent‑front (G595R, G623R), and xDFG (G667S, G696A) positions emerge at progression on larotrectinib, sterically hindering drug binding while preserving kinase activity and causing clinical resistance across TRK fusion–positive cancers, including lung.

Row9.Pathway ID/Name: Bypass MAPK/PI3K activation via alternative oncogenic drivers (e.g., KRAS, EGFR, other RTKs)
Row9.Rationale: Off‑target resistance mechanisms to TRK inhibitors include activation of bypass pathways; in TRK‑driven tumors, emergence of KRAS mutations and other MAPK activators has been documented after TRK inhibitor failure, suggesting that in NSCLC similar MAPK/PI3K reactivation can maintain growth despite TRK blockade.

Row10.Pathway ID/Name: TRK pathway in neurogenic tumor–nerve crosstalk
Row10.Rationale: Cancer‑neuroscience data highlight TRK (especially TrkB) as a mediator of anoikis resistance and metastatic spread; larotrectinib and entrectinib are proposed to disrupt NGF/BDNF–TRK signaling that supports invasion and survival, a mechanism likely relevant to innervated lung tumors with NTRK fusions.

Row11.Rationale: Next‑generation TRK inhibitors were designed to retain potency against solvent‑front and xDFG TRK mutants that confer larotrectinib resistance; selitrectinib and repotrectinib show clinical and preclinical activity in patients with secondary NTRK mutations, restoring suppression of TRK‑driven MAPK/PI3K signaling, including in thoracic malignancies.

Row12.Pathway ID/Name: Multi‑kinase TRK/ROS1/ALK signaling in lung cancer (entrectinib and related TKIs)
Row12.Rationale: Entrectinib inhibits TRK, ROS1, and ALK with low‑nanomolar potency and has substantial activity in ROS1‑rearranged and NTRK‑fusion NSCLC, including brain metastases; however, kinase‑domain mutations (e.g., NTRK3 G623R) and bypass signaling can confer resistance, informing sequencing after larotrectinib failure.
